description: Baseline benchmark run with complex scenarios across text sizes, commands, and JSON walker.

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement. Primary target: 4KB text pipeline (~701µs/op). Clean text (~494µs/op) reveals regex overhead is dominant even with zero matches.

description: Add probe-based fast paths to scanFreeText and scanKnownTokens, plus entropy fast-path. Clean text 60% faster; 4KB text 3% faster.

asi.hypothesis: Pre-filter regex layers with cheap substring/digit probes to avoid regex backtracking on inputs with no candidates. Biggest win on clean text where no patterns match.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reducing per-layer string allocations and optimizing scanKnownTokens which is still ~20% of runtime on PII-rich inputs.

description: Skip sort.Slice for 0-1 intervals and fast-path applyIntervals for single interval.

asi.hypothesis: Avoid sort.Slice and strings.Builder overhead for common case of 0-1 matches per layer.
asi.next_action_hint: Profile PII-rich input to find next bottleneck. Consider reducing string copies between layers or optimizing scanKnownTokens regex ordering.

description: Single-pass interval collection for KindText. Slight speedup but 69% more allocations because all layers scan original input, finding more matches that later get discarded.

asi.hypothesis: Collect intervals from all text layers on original input and apply once to avoid intermediate string copies.
asi.next_action_hint: Try a hybrid: run Layer 0+1 sequentially, then combine Layers 3+4 on the same string. Or focus on reducing per-match allocations in existing scanners.
asi.rollback_reason: Allocation regression: 115k allocs/op vs 68k baseline. All layers scanning original input find ~2x more matches that get discarded by overlap resolution. Net speedup is small (1.2%) and doesn't justify the GC pressure.

description: Combine Layers 3+4 into single pass. Small speedup (1.2%) but 30% more allocations because entropy layer scans pre-free-text-redacted text, finding extra overlapping matches that get discarded.

asi.hypothesis: Combine free-text and entropy layers into single pass to avoid one string copy.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reducing regex engine load: remove redundant patterns, optimize expensive regexes, or batch candidate discovery.
asi.rollback_reason: 30% allocation increase. scanEntropy on pre-redacted text finds extra matches that overlap with free-text matches and get discarded by applyIntervals.

description: Re-apply pre-sizing for interval slices + hand-rolled JWT scanner. Marginal 4KB improvement (~0.3%) but command benchmark improved 5.7%. Keep as stepping stone for further regex optimizations.

asi.hypothesis: Hand-rolled JWT scanner + pre-sized slices. JWT scanner saves regex backtracking but slice pre-sizing hurt low-match cases. Overall small win.
asi.next_action_hint: Split long alternation patterns (especially aws_access_key) into separate probed patterns to reduce regex engine work.

description: Replace regex-based entropy scanner with hand-rolled linear scanner. Eliminates regexp backtracker allocations and CPU overhead for token matching.

asi.hypothesis: Hand-rolled entropy scanner avoids regex engine overhead (backtracking + slice allocations) by walking token runs directly.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for other regex layers that can be hand-rolled: credit_card in scanFreeText, or scanKnownTokens patterns with complex backtracking.

description: Replace credit card regex with hand-rolled scanner that walks digit sequences and validates Luhn directly. Avoids regex backtracking on complex quantified pattern.

asi.hypothesis: Hand-rolled credit card scanner avoids regex backtracking on `(?:\d[ \-]?){12,18}\d` which was a major CPU and allocation source in scanFreeText.
asi.next_action_hint: Run fresh CPU profile to identify next bottleneck. Likely scanKnownTokens (50% before) or remaining scanFreeText regexes (iban, ipv4).

description: Add probes for discord_bot_token and asana_pat to skip expensive regexes on inputs without candidates.

asi.hypothesis: discord_bot_token and asana_pat had no probes, so their expensive regexes ran on every input. Adding simple substring/digit probes skips them on most inputs.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue hand-rolling expensive regexes in scanKnownTokens (uri_credentials) and scanFreeText (ipv4_public). Run fresh CPU profile.

description: Remove uri_credentials regex from scanKnownTokens because findConnURIs in Layer 0 already handles the same shape with a faster hand-rolled scanner.

asi.hypothesis: uri_credentials regex was a redundant safety net that almost never matched (findConnURIs caught everything first). Removing it saves an expensive regex scan on every input with :// and @.
asi.next_action_hint: Hand-roll scanFormats multiline regexes (dotenv_pair, yaml_pair, http_header) which are expensive due to line-start anchors and long alternations.

description: Hand-roll scanFormats multiline regexes (dotenv, yaml, http_header) into line-by-line string scanners. Eliminates expensive regex backtracking on line-start anchors.

asi.hypothesis: Multiline regexes with (?mi)^ anchors scan every byte looking for line starts. Hand-rolling into line-by-line scanners using strings.IndexByte and simple prefix checks is dramatically faster.
asi.next_action_hint: Run fresh CPU profile. scanKnownTokens (~35%) and scanFreeText (~27%) are likely the next targets. Consider hand-rolling more patterns.
